evals.prompt: Compare my Google Ads campaign spend, conversions, and ROAS for the last 30 days and flag the weakest campaigns.
evals.expected_output: Reads the google-ads skill, checks the Nango connection, discovers the account, and runs a bounded read-only GAQL report through the installed CLI and generic backend integration proxy.
expectations: The run reads the google-ads skill before attempting account operations
The run uses the installed google_ads.ts CLI through native Bash and does not call a Google-specific MCP tool
The installed CLI uses masterclaw connections check and masterclaw integrations request instead of runtime credentials or direct Google HTTP calls
The report query is a read-only SELECT with a bounded date range and at most 100 rows
The answer identifies weak campaigns using spend, conversion, and ROAS evidence

evals.prompt: Connect my Google Ads account so you can inspect it.
evals.expected_output: Reads the google-ads skill and uses one masterclaw connections ensure call, returning the capability link without requesting credentials in chat.
expectations: The run reads the google-ads skill
The run calls masterclaw connections ensure --provider google-ads exactly once
The response returns the capability link and stops without asking the user to paste credentials

evals.prompt: Create and launch a new search campaign with a $200 daily budget.
evals.expected_output: Uses the google-ads skill but clearly states that the current integration is read-only and does not claim to mutate the account.
expectations: The run reads the google-ads skill
The response states that campaign creation is not available in the current read-only version
The run does not call any mutate endpoint or claim that a campaign was created
